This months' movie:  Wish Asha, a sharp-witted idealist, makes a wish so powerful that it is answered by a cosmic force, a little ball of boundless energy called Star. Together, Asha and Star confront a most formidable foe the ruler of Rosas, King Magnifico to save her community and prove that when the will of one courageous human connects with the magic of the stars, wondrous things can happen.

events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.description: Drop-in program . A preschool readiness program that builds independence and confidence through circle time, guided play, early learning activities, and open ended art. events.title: Rainbow Day: Wear Your Rainbow!
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled hour of creativity to celebrate Pride! Decorate your own t-shirt, tote bag, and/or bandana with a variety of fabric paints and markers. Express yourself, show your pride, and create something uniquely yours in this crafting program for the whole family. All materials will be provided while supplies last. Participants are also welcome to bring their own items from home to decorate. For children and their families. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Nature Explorers
events.description: Drop-in program. Explore spring through outdoor play and nature-based activities. Children may plant seeds, touch leaves, soil, water, sand, go on simple scavenger hunts, and create nature-inspired open ended art. Dress for outdoor play when possible. This program is co-sponsored with ACSA.

events.title: Yoga Storytime
events.description: Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Join Megan Snider, author and Founder of Appleseed Yoga for Kids as she delivers inspiring stories and gets the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or material

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

The program is safe, supervised, and facilitated in partnership with North York Community House LEAP staff. events.title: Hands-On Introduction to Robots Sensors Programming
events.description: Gain hands-on experience on how robots use their sensors to work using Scribbler Robots, then test how their programming allows these robots to respond to their environments. events.title: Author Visit: Jane and the Blue Willow Princess with Catherine Little
events.description: “On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​” Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.

events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis.  Suitable for ages 6-12. Dash: May 3, June 7, July 5, August 9 LEGO: May 10, June 14, July 12, August 16 Snap Circuits: May 24, June 21, July 19, August 23 Sphero: May 31, June 28, July 26, August 30

events.title: Board Games for Kids
events.description: Jenga! Monopoly! Ticket to Ride! And More! Spend the afternoon playing different board games with your friends!  Snacks are offered during this program for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: SUNDAY FUN-DAY
events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: Sunday SNAP Circuits
events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.title: Curious Chaos Club
events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.title: Sunday STEAM at the Riverdale Branch
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits, LEGO, K'nex, Marble Run and Kids Craft Club. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Make a craft you can take home. Ages 4 and up.

events.title: Sunday Fun STEAM
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits,.. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in: No registration required.

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sundays at the Movies
events.description: Join us for an afternoon at the movies – enjoy some of your favorite films at the library! Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Pokémon Club
events.description: Whether you're a new or expert Pokémon Trainer, everyone is welcome! Show off your Pokémon knowledge and research skills and join our exciting Pokémon scavenger hunt for free Pokémon cards! Feel free to bring in your old Pokémon cards to play with or trade with staff and other Pokémon trainers!

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: This month we will play with electricity and circuitry. Turn on a light, spin a motor, activate a speaker and choose from hundreds of different projects, or try making your own circuits! For ages 6-12. Registration is required. Parent/Caregiver must attend. "Mizore is shy and soft-spoken, while her best friend Nozomi is outgoing. The two girls grow closer as they practice for their last duet before graduating high school. events.title: Youth Hub: Boba Making with Chef Yumiko

events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

events.title: Family Fibers

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.title: Sir Henry Pellatt and the English Trip of 1910

This lively and fascinating account of a military expedition of over 600 Toronto men to Edwardian England, was paid for by Sir Henry Pellatt, the wealthy Canadian financier who later realized his dream of building the Casa Loma castle. events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Ringed Planet Cross Stitch
events.description: Join us at the Merril Collection for an introductory cross stitch workshop. Participants will stitch a two-colour ringed planet. Enjoy our current exhibit, Solar System Stories , and learn a new skill. This workshop is designed for beginners with no sewing or cross stitch experience. All supplies will be provided. events.title: Intro to D&D for Teens
events.description: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons ! This is a drop-in, learn-to-play program for T eens . 1st Level Pre-generated character available. All supplies provided. Adventure: The Peculiar Case of the Selptan Felines Friday July 24, 2-4pm Aug 7, 2-4pm Space limited, but watchers are welcome. events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Open Mic at Toronto Reference Library (Music, Poetry & Storytelling)
events.description: Are you a musician, poet, or storyteller? If so, then you are invited to share your talents at the Toronto Reference Library Open Mic! Please note: stand-up comedy is no longer featured at this event. Guitar and piano provided. 5 minutes for each performance. Program is for adults, teens, and seniors. Sign-up is at 5:30 pm. events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around  the Branch Tour
events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood walk. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism.
